unit_27 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_27

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 293 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Space is often referred to as the final frontier, as the only realm of which humankind has still to gain substantial understanding, yet the ocean is also another vast area about which our knowledge is __________.Space is often referred to as the final frontier, as the only realm of which humankind has still to gain substantial understanding, yet the ocean is also another vast area about which our knowledge is __________.
答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy
frustratefrustratingrealm
frustrate: standard unit headword | frustrating: standard unit headword | realm: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　reticent; discretion
solitudereticent
solitude: standard unit headword | reticent: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented
reviewexercise
review: standard unit headword | exercise: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
Researchers have deduced that infants come equipped with some simple expectations about how objects behave, __________ of which solicit their rapt attention: babies stare longer when events appear to defy the laws of physics.Researchers have deduced that infants come equipped with some simple expectations about how objects behave, __________ of which solicit their rapt attention: babies stare longer when events appear to defy the laws of physics.
答案：B　violations
appeardeduce
appear: standard unit headword | deduce: standard unit headword
#5 SE
Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive
out ofassembly
out of: standard unit headword | assembly: standard unit headword
#6 TC DOUBLE
The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　verbal nimbleness; plodding
verbal
verbal: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
archivepreyrealm
archive: standard unit headword | prey: standard unit headword | realm: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Some novelists immodestly idealized and exaggerate the significance of their work, but others, __________ to exalt the role of the writer, question a transcendent view of the art.Some novelists immodestly idealized and exaggerate the significance of their work, but others, __________ to exalt the role of the writer, question a transcendent view of the art.
答案：B　declining

#9 SE

答案：C, D　respite; relief
relief

#10 SE
The first images of Jupiter’s moon Callisto show bright regions of material, as if older and darker ice had slid downhill and exposed the __________ ice underneath.The first images of Jupiter’s moon Callisto show bright regions of material, as if older and darker ice had slid downhill and exposed the __________ ice underneath.
答案：D, F　pristine; unblemished
compactas if

Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　complained; a misnomer; a transformation
misnomer
misnomer: standard unit headword

Parker's model of human reflects a __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally pessimistic analyses of her colleagues in the economics department.Parker's model of human reflects a __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally pessimistic analyses of her colleagues in the economics department.
答案：E　sanguine
stark
stark: standard unit headword
#14 TC DOUBLE
Prior to the advent of the scientific method, many were skeptical of experimentation as a means for seeking knowledge. It was widely thought that (i) __________ could only produce (ii) __________ findings.Prior to the advent of the scientific method, many were skeptical of experimentation as a means for seeking knowledge. It was widely thought that (i) __________ could only produce (ii) __________ findings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　artificially produced experiences; spurious

#15 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity
disabusereview
disabuse: standard unit headword | review: standard unit headword

There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are __________; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are __________; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.
答案：B, D　analogous; comparable
comparable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic
albeit

#19 TC TRIPLE
Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　humanly audible; attenuate; astonished by
attenuate
attenuate: standard unit headword
#20 TC SINGLE
The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the __________ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the __________ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.
答案：B　wherewithal
snake
snake: standard unit headword
#21 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
steep
steep: standard unit headword
#22 TC DOUBLE
There has been (i) __________ elephant's fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii) __________ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.There has been (i) __________ elephant's fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii) __________ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　only anecdotal evidence for; buttressed
buttress
buttress: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　relished; delighted in
trickster

答案：E　obtrude into
on the part of
on the part of: standard unit headword
#25 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
in the midst of
in the midst of: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　Projecting; Extrapolating
extrapolate

Blank 2
答案：B, E　kill; consistent with

Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　difficulty of; still more acute; specious
acute
acute: standard unit headword

答案：D　unqualified

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

Scientists now recognize that the human brain has remarkable plasticity and potential for restoring lost function. Following injuries to the brain that cause permanent but localized damage to brain tissue, neurological deficits can often be (i) __________ by special training. In other words, changes in the brain can be (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：C, D　ameliorated; electively produced
localize

But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion

Proving the forest-climate link through verifiable and experimental scientific means rather than observation was (ii)__________ for these individuals, a situation that eventually led to the link (iii)__________ justifications for forest conservation.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　employed; problematic; dropping out of
out of

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering
comforting
comforting: standard unit headword
#51 SE
Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.
答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic
abased
abased: standard unit headword
#52 TC TRIPLE
The prime minister underestimates the fatigue that her continual (i)__________ induces, even among those who are largely (ii)__________ her aims. Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.The prime minister underestimates the fatigue that her continual (i)__________ induces, even among those who are largely (ii)__________ her aims. Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　overclaiming; sympathetic to; radically transforming
sympathetic
sympathetic: standard unit headword
#53 SE
Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two do share a family history.Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two do share a family history.
答案：D, E　preclude; rule out
signify
signify: standard unit headword
#54 TC SINGLE
We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of __________.We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of __________.
答案：A　reliability
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#55 TC SINGLE
The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a __________ exercise in fiscal prudence.The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a __________ exercise in fiscal prudence.
答案：C　specious
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#56 TC DOUBLE
Svitlana Mayboroda's recent work, though (i) __________ pure mathematics, is already being (ii) __________ by physicists, distinguishing it from most mathematical advances, for which the development of any practical use is long delayed.Svitlana Mayboroda's recent work, though (i) __________ pure mathematics, is already being (ii) __________ by physicists, distinguishing it from most mathematical advances, for which the development of any practical use is long delayed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　in the realm of; applied
realm
realm: standard unit headword

The novel presents him as a man who monitors his state of mind and emotions as though he were doing so __________, talking and thinking about himself as if he were someone else.The novel presents him as a man who monitors his state of mind and emotions as though he were doing so __________, talking and thinking about himself as if he were someone else.
答案：E　externally
as if
as if: standard unit headword
#58 TC SINGLE
Slight but __________ variations in the timing of the star’s light pulses led astronomers to deduce that it was being pulled backward and forward by three planets orbiting around it.Slight but __________ variations in the timing of the star’s light pulses led astronomers to deduce that it was being pulled backward and forward by three planets orbiting around it.
答案：B　regular

#59 TC TRIPLE

To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i) __________, mathematicians are (ii) __________ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　reliable; skeptical regarding; appear to work

#60 TC SINGLE

答案：E　eschewed
seek
seek: standard unit headword
#61 TC SINGLE
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis

seek: standard unit headword
#62 TC DOUBLE
It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　frustrating; conformed to

答案：E　follow
comparable

#64 TC SINGLE

答案：E　unexploited
prey

#65 TC DOUBLE
Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate

Conversely, leaders who set goals that are credible extensions of current realities can seem unimaginative.The more (i) __________ the future goal advocated by an organization's leader, the more (ii) __________ it becomes in relation to the status quo, and therefore the greater will be the tendency of followers to perceive the leader as (iii) __________. Conversely, leaders who set goals that are credible extensions of current realities can seem unimaginative.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　idealized; discrepant; visionary

At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.
答案：A, D　blithe; winsome

答案：A　synonym for

答案：C, F　thwarted; frustrated

Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous

答案：C　predominant

Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

答案：A　incontrovertible

Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. 答案：A, F　accession to; acquiescence to

#102 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

out of: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i) __________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii) __________.Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i) __________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
On its surface, Laszlo Krasznahorkai's novel appears allegorical, but his works tend to construct allegories only to __________ them.On its surface, Laszlo Krasznahorkai's novel appears allegorical, but his works tend to construct allegories only to __________ them.
答案：A　raze
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#105 SE
In mathematics, judgments about the validity of proofs are mediated by peer-reviewed journals; to ensure __________, reviewers are carefully chosen by journal editors, and the identities of scholars whose papers are under consideration are kept secret.In mathematics, judgments about the validity of proofs are mediated by peer-reviewed journals; to ensure __________, reviewers are carefully chosen by journal editors, and the identities of scholars whose papers are under consideration are kept secret.
答案：C, E　fairness; objectivity
review
review: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
seek
seek: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation
on the part of
on the part of: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Some experts now feel that exercise should not be regarded as __________ other treatments for depression: they believe that exercise can itself serve as an appropriate remedy for some forms of the mood disorder.Some experts now feel that exercise should not be regarded as __________ other treatments for depression: they believe that exercise can itself serve as an appropriate remedy for some forms of the mood disorder.
答案：D　an adjunct to
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
solitary
solitary: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Until the advent of film, commercial entertainment in England occurred only where concentrated urban populations provided audiences large enough to make it remunerative: theaters and music halls were (i) __________ in rural villages. But village cinemas quickly became (ii) __________, even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.Until the advent of film, commercial entertainment in England occurred only where concentrated urban populations provided audiences large enough to make it remunerative: theaters and music halls were (i) __________ in rural villages. But village cinemas quickly became (ii) __________, even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　unconceivable; commonplace
advent
advent: standard unit headword
#112 TC SINGLE
The development of fracture mechanics marked a crucial advance in our understanding of crack growth in glasses and ceramics; before the advent of this discipline, many of the details of the crack growth process were __________ those who studied ceramics.The development of fracture mechanics marked a crucial advance in our understanding of crack growth in glasses and ceramics; before the advent of this discipline, many of the details of the crack growth process were __________ those who studied ceramics.
答案：C　opaque to
advent
advent: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.
答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky

out of: standard unit headword

Parker’s model of human affairs reflects (i)__________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii)__________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)__________ theirs.Parker’s model of human affairs reflects (i)__________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii)__________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)__________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly from
stark
stark: standard unit headword
#115 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impediment; naiveté
on the part of
on the part of: standard unit headword
#116 TC SINGLE
Before divulging the nature of their experiments, the researchers anticipated __________, thinking that their work might appear macabre; hence they prepared carefully for a press conference at which they emphasized the project's scientific and medical value.Before divulging the nature of their experiments, the researchers anticipated __________, thinking that their work might appear macabre; hence they prepared carefully for a press conference at which they emphasized the project's scientific and medical value.
答案：B　adverse repercussions

appear: standard unit headword
#117 SE
An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so __________.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depressing; diverting

答案：E　polemic

答案：B, F　canned; formulaic

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic

答案：B　severe

答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.

答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

#128 TC SINGLE
Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.
答案：A　an exogenous
solitary
solitary: standard unit headword
#129 SE

答案：B, D　expansion; augmentation

Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.The (i)__________ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　inert; wonderfully enriched; circumvent

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　little errors; minor

答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; a staleness

Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii) __________ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.Fears that the recent sharp rise in oil prices is an indication that oil is running out appear to be (i) __________. The Middle East still contains vast oil supplies. Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii) __________ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　unfounded; less frequent

答案：A, D　flexibility; suppleness

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

But this agricultural archive is (i) __________ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world's most (ii) __________ places.

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i) __________ meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account

Blank 2

Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.
答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious

#156 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
in the midst of
in the midst of: standard unit headword
#157 TC DOUBLE
To function as (i) __________, literary critics must write well. A badly written book review is worse than a badly written political speech or greeting card; a badly written review is (ii) __________, like a barber with a terrible haircut. If critics cannot write well, how can they authoritatively recognize and promote good writing?To function as (i) __________, literary critics must write well. A badly written book review is worse than a badly written political speech or greeting card; a badly written review is (ii) __________, like a barber with a terrible haircut. If critics cannot write well, how can they authoritatively recognize and promote good writing?
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　evangelists; self-defeating
review
review: standard unit headword
#158 SE
Travel writers have a special burden of forming __________ story—that is, one with a beginning, middle, and end—out of the unformed moments that make up the act of traveling.Travel writers have a special burden of forming __________ story—that is, one with a beginning, middle, and end—out of the unformed moments that make up the act of traveling.
答案：B, E　a coherent; an orderly
out of
out of: standard unit headword
#159 TC TRIPLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　centralizing; raise doubts about; preclude
assembly
assembly: standard unit headword

Seemingly to compensate for the (i) __________ nature of her subject, in her review the theater critic (ii) __________ a casual chatty style.Seemingly to compensate for the (i) __________ nature of her subject, in her review the theater critic (ii) __________ a casual chatty style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rarefied; opted for
review
review: standard unit headword
#161 SE
Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.
答案：A, B　minimal; trifling
appear
appear: standard unit headword

Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly

stark: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　opprobrium; nuanced
sympathetic
sympathetic: standard unit headword
#164 SE
In 1884, Sewall and Dow agreed to join Roosevelt in the Dakota territory for reasons that appear to have been __________: Sewell later recalled that Roosevelt guaranteed them a share of anything made in his cattle business.In 1884, Sewall and Dow agreed to join Roosevelt in the Dakota territory for reasons that appear to have been __________: Sewell later recalled that Roosevelt guaranteed them a share of anything made in his cattle business.
答案：A, C　pecuniary; economic
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#165 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
solitary
solitary: standard unit headword
#166 TC DOUBLE
An affinity for (i) __________ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii) __________ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.An affinity for (i) __________ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii) __________ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　paradox; unresolvable ambivalences
appear
appear: standard unit headword

Estimating demographic parameters in marine mammals is challenging, often requiring many years of data to achieve sufficient precision to __________ biologically meaningful change.Estimating demographic parameters in marine mammals is challenging, often requiring many years of data to achieve sufficient precision to __________ biologically meaningful change.
答案：C, F　discern; detect
demographic
demographic: standard unit headword

Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself

seek: standard unit headword

Lohr's (i) __________ the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) __________, celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.Lohr's (i) __________ the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) __________, celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　refusal to wear; mysterious
albeit
albeit: standard unit headword
#170 SE
Natural aging causes nerves to die off and muscles to weaken, but regular exercise enlarges muscle fibers and will help __________ the decline by increasing the strength in muscles you have left.Natural aging causes nerves to die off and muscles to weaken, but regular exercise enlarges muscle fibers and will help __________ the decline by increasing the strength in muscles you have left.
答案：B, C　stave off; forestall

exercise: standard unit headword
#171 TC TRIPLE
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#172 TC DOUBLE
An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　unfettered originality; solipsistic
realm
realm: standard unit headword
#173 SE

答案：A, F　ceded to; left to
realm
realm: standard unit headword
#174 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
steep
steep: standard unit headword
#175 TC DOUBLE
When Marx wrote, "the philosophers have only interpreted the world in various ways; the point, however, is to change it," he was not only (i) __________ philosophers. He was (ii) __________ interpretation itself, as if thinking were an idle affair compared with action, where real people make their mark on the world.When Marx wrote, "the philosophers have only interpreted the world in various ways; the point, however, is to change it," he was not only (i) __________ philosophers. He was (ii) __________ interpretation itself, as if thinking were an idle affair compared with action, where real people make their mark on the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　slighting; disparaging

答案：C, D　irresolution; vacillation

stark: standard unit headword
#177 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

out of: standard unit headword
#178 TC DOUBLE
She was skeptical about the (i) __________ reviews of the latest production of the play, feeling that the production she had seen years earlier was (ii) __________, and that any successive attempts must undoubtedly fall short.She was skeptical about the (i) __________ reviews of the latest production of the play, feeling that the production she had seen years earlier was (ii) __________, and that any successive attempts must undoubtedly fall short.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rhapsodic; nothing less than definitive

答案：A, D　flexibility; suppleness

The country appears to be getting (ii)__________ even as its stream flow becomes less (iii)__________.The amount of water flowing through United States streams has (i)__________ during this century, although without giving rise to more frequent floods, according to a new study by the United States Geological Survey. The country appears to be getting (ii)__________ even as its stream flow becomes less (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　steadily increased; wetter; destructive

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　plainspoken; unadorned

The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.
答案：C, E　exhaustive; comprehensive

Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　aped; instructive; doomed
triumph

There is no (i) __________ in developing a scientific theory that is (ii) __________; rather, the generation, testing, and frequently, discarding of new ideas must be regarded as an essential part of the process by which scientists gain the knowledge and understanding they seek.There is no (i) __________ in developing a scientific theory that is (ii) __________; rather, the generation, testing, and frequently, discarding of new ideas must be regarded as an essential part of the process by which scientists gain the knowledge and understanding they seek.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　shame; eventually refuted

答案：A, C　reveal; manifest

Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.It is a common complaint that female explorers have been unfairly (i)__________ in modern historical accounts. Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ignored; well enough known; prejudice

Adapted (i) __________, prairie grasses have developed roots that are primed for (ii) __________: if a plant's stem is chewed down even to near ground level, it will shoot right back up again, as if defying discouragement.Adapted (i) __________, prairie grasses have developed roots that are primed for (ii) __________: if a plant's stem is chewed down even to near ground level, it will shoot right back up again, as if defying discouragement.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　to survive grazing; regeneration

albeit

It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　continuation; compensatory mechanism; guaranteed

答案：C, F　homegrown; indigenous

Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　degree of gruesome detail; sensationalism; underscoring

As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.The prioritization by academic institutions of scholarly publication as a criterion for advancement in science departments discourages researchers from sharing their data with peers. As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.

Blank 2

It was long believed that when both lions and hyenas were present at a kill, the latter invariably stole from the former, but this notion proved to be (ii) __________: we now know that the lion is just as (iii) __________ the hard-won prey of the hyena.The unattractive hyena is unjustly (i) __________. It was long believed that when both lions and hyenas were present at a kill, the latter invariably stole from the former, but this notion proved to be (ii) __________: we now know that the lion is just as (iii) __________ the hard-won prey of the hyena.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　maligned; untrue; apt to pilfer

答案：C, E　highlighting; underscoring

Blank 2

Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.In general, observational studies tend to (i) __________ the effect of diet on overall health because a healthy diet is often (ii) __________ a healthy lifestyle. People who watch what they eat probably exercise more, avoid smoking, and follow other behaviors (iii) __________ good health. Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, E　sacrosanct; inviolable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undermine; camouflage

It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.There is a powerful tradition within French writing that has (i)__________ the bounds of conventional morality, (ii)__________ the dictates of Enlightenment reason, and expressed (iii)__________ violence. It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　challenged; defied; an abiding fascination with

答案：A, C　parochial; narrow

Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) __________.

#203 TC TRIPLE
Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added
prey
prey: standard unit headword
#204 SE
Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.
答案：A, F　uncontrolled; random
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#205 TC DOUBLE
Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i) __________ events, whereas weeds are (ii) __________.Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i) __________ events, whereas weeds are (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous
out of
out of: standard unit headword
#206 TC TRIPLE
The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　self-serving; self-regard; self-aggrandizement
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#207 SE
Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly __________: prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly __________: prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.
答案：B, D　opportunistic; improvisational
prey
prey: standard unit headword
#208 TC DOUBLE
That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　impede; cerebral
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#209 TC TRIPLE
Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#210 SE
In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.
答案：A, B　vapid; bland
in the midst of
in the midst of: standard unit headword
#211 TC DOUBLE
Although there is certainly no (i) __________ for overcoming (ii) __________ mind-set among students, there are numerous materials, approaches, and exercises that, if used creatively, can help students gain glimpses of how other individuals think of themselves and see their worlds.Although there is certainly no (i) __________ for overcoming (ii) __________ mind-set among students, there are numerous materials, approaches, and exercises that, if used creatively, can help students gain glimpses of how other individuals think of themselves and see their worlds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　panacea; an insular
exercise
exercise: standard unit headword
#212 TC TRIPLE
Researchers found that people who had just watched film clips of views from an airplane window were much more (i) __________ in computer games than people who had watched clips showing scenes from a car window. The same relationship between (ii) __________ and (iii) __________ behavior appeared in several experiments. The researchers suggest that being high up, or the mere act of ascending, reminds us of lofty ways of thinking and behaving.Researchers found that people who had just watched film clips of views from an airplane window were much more (i) __________ in computer games than people who had watched clips showing scenes from a car window. The same relationship between (ii) __________ and (iii) __________ behavior appeared in several experiments. The researchers suggest that being high up, or the mere act of ascending, reminds us of lofty ways of thinking and behaving.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　cooperative; altitude; unselfish
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#213 SE
As if addressing an academic seminar rather than a judicial hearing, the newspaper executive turned __________, pondering not just her own career but also the future of the press in the digital era.As if addressing an academic seminar rather than a judicial hearing, the newspaper executive turned __________, pondering not just her own career but also the future of the press in the digital era.
答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative
as if
as if: standard unit headword
#214 TC DOUBLE
Recent research suggests that social complexity and social intelligence are widespread among monkeys and apes. The use and manufacture of tools, by contrast, is (i) __________ two of the great apes, chimpanzees and orangutans, and tool use among monkeys appears to be quite (ii) __________.Recent research suggests that social complexity and social intelligence are widespread among monkeys and apes. The use and manufacture of tools, by contrast, is (i) __________ two of the great apes, chimpanzees and orangutans, and tool use among monkeys appears to be quite (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　restricted almost entirely to; rudimentary

The label "melodrama" tends to have (i) __________ implications when used to describe a film with historical subject matter. Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii) __________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　pejorative; a seemingly unmediated; objectivity

答案：A, B　exile; separation

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　write poorly; engaging

#218 TC TRIPLE
The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle

seek: standard unit headword
#219 SE
When philosophers like Plato, Hume, and Schopenhauer write with clarity about problems of the utmost difficulty, such __________ does not make the problems appear simple or easy to solve: on the contrary, difficulty is exposed as essential to the understanding.When philosophers like Plato, Hume, and Schopenhauer write with clarity about problems of the utmost difficulty, such __________ does not make the problems appear simple or easy to solve: on the contrary, difficulty is exposed as essential to the understanding.
答案：A, D　lucidity; intelligibility

#220 TC DOUBLE
Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; opprobrium

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　obscure the paucity; recondite; disguise

Unlike Chopin, whose etudes are musically sophisticated and adventurous, Czerny conceived his exercises as __________ works.Unlike Chopin, whose etudes are musically sophisticated and adventurous, Czerny conceived his exercises as __________ works.
答案：B, E　functional; utilitarian

exercise: standard unit headword
#223 TC DOUBLE
The grinding stones featured in some of Puerto Rico's restaurants as part of their decor evoke an idealized rustic past, but the nostalgia is (i) __________: in reality, the job of grinding corn was among the most (ii) __________ of domestic chores, often performed by the women of the house.The grinding stones featured in some of Puerto Rico's restaurants as part of their decor evoke an idealized rustic past, but the nostalgia is (i) __________: in reality, the job of grinding corn was among the most (ii) __________ of domestic chores, often performed by the women of the house.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　misplaced; onerous

The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

appear: standard unit headword

Because modern-day crinoids usually lose and regenerate their arms as a result of attacks by other marine animals, scientists have reasoned that evidence of arm regeneration in fossil crinoids might be __________ of past predator-prey interactions.Because modern-day crinoids usually lose and regenerate their arms as a result of attacks by other marine animals, scientists have reasoned that evidence of arm regeneration in fossil crinoids might be __________ of past predator-prey interactions.
答案：E, F　an indicator; a hint

Although movie critic Pauline Kael had a distaste for sycophancy, he also had the need for (i)__________, and as a consequence of his competing feelings, he often showed (ii)__________ signals to his relatives and friends.Although movie critic Pauline Kael had a distaste for sycophancy, he also had the need for (i)__________, and as a consequence of his competing feelings, he often showed (ii)__________ signals to his relatives and friends.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　obeisance; mixed

#227 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
idealism
idealism: standard unit headword

While dehydration during pregnancy does not affect aspic vipers' litter size, pregnant females lost 89 percent more mass than non-reproductive females did when water-deprived, suggesting that female snakes may __________ their own water balance to benefit their offspring.While dehydration during pregnancy does not affect aspic vipers' litter size, pregnant females lost 89 percent more mass than non-reproductive females did when water-deprived, suggesting that female snakes may __________ their own water balance to benefit their offspring.
答案：A, D　compromise; sacrifice

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　failure; indifference to
