It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude

appear: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　belied; contradicted

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　propitious; a disadvantageous choice; limited

答案：C, F　stimulated; spurred

The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
verbal

答案：B, D　striking; pronounced

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　an adherence to; tame; embraces

Because Mars is smaller than Earth, it cooled faster, and therefore it lost the interrial heat that drives tectonic activity.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　moribund; vital

#248 TC TRIPLE
Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　numbing; lambast; obviate
on the part of
on the part of: standard unit headword
#249 SE
Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to __________ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to __________ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　softened; incompatible
review

#251 TC TRIPLE
Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#252 SE
Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.
答案：B, E　galvanize; animate
frustrate
frustrate: standard unit headword
#253 TC DOUBLE
Professor Redfield describes in his recent book publishing as dark as it is deep, even as one hidebound industry. Indeed, it is facing a profound change in its product model, but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunities offered by technological change.Professor Redfield describes in his recent book publishing as dark as it is deep, even as one hidebound industry. Indeed, it is facing a profound change in its product model, but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunities offered by technological change.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　encumbered by; unable to seize
seek
seek: standard unit headword
#254 TC TRIPLE
Climbing mountains in search of an awe-inspiring view can be (i) __________ experience. Too often, the peak seems forever out of reach, always disappearing over the next crest. It's a feeling that (ii) __________ physicists, who have spent much of the last century trudging up a seemingly endless slope in search of truly cosmic vistas. It is proving (iii) __________ climb.Climbing mountains in search of an awe-inspiring view can be (i) __________ experience. Too often, the peak seems forever out of reach, always disappearing over the next crest. It's a feeling that (ii) __________ physicists, who have spent much of the last century trudging up a seemingly endless slope in search of truly cosmic vistas. It is proving (iii) __________ climb.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　a disappointing; must be shared by; a difficult
out of
out of: standard unit headword
#255 SE
Sports stars are often __________ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.Sports stars are often __________ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.
答案：A, F　totemic; emblematic
idealize
idealize: standard unit headword
#256 TC DOUBLE
One example of a (i) __________ occurs in a vacuum state in quantum field theory. There, “something” and “nothing” are (ii) __________: it is entirely consistent for there to be nothing, and nonetheless for stuff to show up when we try to detect it.One example of a (i) __________ occurs in a vacuum state in quantum field theory. There, “something” and “nothing” are (ii) __________: it is entirely consistent for there to be nothing, and nonetheless for stuff to show up when we try to detect it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　paradox; not mutually exclusive
misnomer
misnomer: standard unit headword
#257 TC TRIPLE
The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　anonymity; unable to establish; dismiss
seek
seek: standard unit headword
#258 SE
Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely __________, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely __________, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.
答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous
acute
acute: standard unit headword
#259 TC DOUBLE
A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　distinct from; intransigent
comparable
comparable: standard unit headword
#260 TC TRIPLE
In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking
seek
seek: standard unit headword
#261 SE
For decades, Pluto seemed to be the mysteriously __________ planet: it was first thought to be about as large as Earth, but, subsequently, measurements had it smaller and smaller.For decades, Pluto seemed to be the mysteriously __________ planet: it was first thought to be about as large as Earth, but, subsequently, measurements had it smaller and smaller.
答案：C, E　dwindling; contracting
appear
appear: standard unit headword
#262 TC DOUBLE
Most of the bank-efficiency literature suggests that bank mergers culminating in larger, more complex organizations (i) __________ significant economies of scale or scope that are (ii) __________ to consumers. Indeed, some of this literature suggests diseconomies, which would lead to higher loan rates or lower deposit rates for consumers.Most of the bank-efficiency literature suggests that bank mergers culminating in larger, more complex organizations (i) __________ significant economies of scale or scope that are (ii) __________ to consumers. Indeed, some of this literature suggests diseconomies, which would lead to higher loan rates or lower deposit rates for consumers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　fail to provide; beneficial

seek: standard unit headword
#263 TC TRIPLE
It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　repressed; embarrassment; distress
out of
out of: standard unit headword
#264 SE
When the atmosphere over the city is at its best, it is peculiarly __________, and this clarity seems to distill the very special beauty of the place.When the atmosphere over the city is at its best, it is peculiarly __________, and this clarity seems to distill the very special beauty of the place.
答案：A, E　limpid; pellucid

In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constraints

#266 TC TRIPLE
Some scholars have interpreted marks incised on pottery during the Neolithic period, some nearly 6,000 years old, as the (i) __________ of writing. But while the marks do appear to have been meaningful symbols, and while the use of symbols is (ii) __________ writing, there is (iii) __________ that the pottery symbols evolved into a true script.Some scholars have interpreted marks incised on pottery during the Neolithic period, some nearly 6,000 years old, as the (i) __________ of writing. But while the marks do appear to have been meaningful symbols, and while the use of symbols is (ii) __________ writing, there is (iii) __________ that the pottery symbols evolved into a true script.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　forerunner; a prerequisite for; no firm evidence

appear: standard unit headword
#267 SE
During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying __________.During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying __________.
答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.

#271 TC TRIPLE
It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)__________ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)__________ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)__________ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)__________ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)__________ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)__________ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement

acute: standard unit headword
#272 SE
During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.
答案：C, F　undermine; contradict

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　unconnected; superficial; isolationism

Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become __________.Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become __________.
答案：B, D　untenable; indefensible
gratuitous

#275 TC TRIPLE
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity

答案：A, D　affecting; moving

However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt

答案：A, D　judicious; sensible

Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)__________ raw life? He was as vivid physically as he was mentally, almost perversely alert, completely at home in the world of matter, repulsed by (iii)__________.Saul's particular combination of intellectuality and vitality was not paradoxical; it was category-shattering. (i)__________ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)__________ raw life? He was as vivid physically as he was mentally, almost perversely alert, completely at home in the world of matter, repulsed by (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　energy; flush with; tedium

Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Blank 3

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　adopt; threatened to compromise; distinctiveness

答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy

Blank 2
